message:
fix(messaging): make JoinSemilattice::apply_delta return bool and trigger anti-entropy on failure

The previous apply_delta() signature returned () and assumed deltas
always applied cleanly. In practice, a partial delta from a diverged
replica can fail to advance local state, leaving the propagation
protocol stuck without requesting repair.

Changes:
- apply_delta() now returns bool (true = state changed, false = failed)
- delta_is_empty() default method avoids allocating a Default for comparison
- CrdtPropagationReplica::apply() checks the return value and marks
  NeedsAntiEntropy when apply_delta returns false, triggering full-state
  repair on the next anti-entropy round
- All concrete JoinSemilattice impls updated (ReplicaCounter,
  InterestSummary, CursorCheckpoint, LagSketch) to return true
- Additional tests for delta-CRDT propagation protocol convergence
  under concurrent mutations and anti-entropy digest exchange

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
